title: lithocardium bracteatum
intro: Lithocardium bracteatum, also known as Digitalis purpurea or foxglove, is a biennial flowering plant native to Europe, but cultivated in many parts of the world for its medicinal properties. The plant has been used for centuries to treat heart conditions due to its cardiac glycoside content, which helps to regulate heart rhythm and increase cardiac output. The medicinal properties of lithocardium bracteatum make it beneficial for conditions such as atrial fibrillation, congestive heart failure, and angina pectoris, promoting improved cardiovascular health and overall well-being. Herbal preparations of lithocardium bracteatum, often in the form of dried leaves or extracts, are commonly used in traditional medicine to support heart health.
